title: Windows Forensics: AD Domain Controller — Live PowerShell
description: DC01.corp.local saw unusual replication and an unexpected account-create event. Connect to the DC's PowerShell, walk Get-ADUser / Get-ADGroupMember / Get-WinEvent / Get-ADObject AdminSDHolder, reconstruct the Kerberoast → DCSync → privilege-persistence chain.

hints.text: `connect dc01`. Cmdlets are case-insensitive. Start with `Get-ADGroupMember "REDACTED"` and compare against the expected roster.
Kerberoasting leaves a trail of 4769 REDACTED events. `Get-WinEvent -LogName REDACTED | include 4769` shows the ticket-request burst and the Service Name (SPN).

DCSync = privileged-object-access from a non-DC source. The Windows event ID is the same for every object access (Directory Service Access) — DCSync's signature is the Properties GUID 1131f6aa-9c07-...
